summary: British-born Mexican Surrealist painter and author. Most commonly identified by Self-Portrait (Inn of the Dawn Horse) with its hyena and rocking horse, The Giantess (The Guardian of the Egg), and Portrait of Max Ernst. Also a novelist: The Hearing Trumpet, Down Below. Friends with Remedios Varo.

works.name: Self-Portrait (Inn of the Dawn Horse) (1937–1938)
works.description: Shows Carrington sitting in a blue chair reaching toward a lactating hyena, with a rocking horse on the wall. Through the window, a white horse (symbolizing her) gallops freely.

clues.clue: Artist sits in a blue chair near a hyena and a rocking horse; white horse gallops through window (symbolizing her freedom)
examples: this artist painted a self-portrait of her sitting in a blue chair near a hyena
sits in a chair in front of a hyena and a rocking horse. Outside the house, a white horse symbolizing her can be seen
a triple-breasted hyena appears in a self-portrait...that features a rocking horse inside and a white horse outside

works.name: The Giantess (The Guardian of the Egg)
works.description: Tempera painting of a massive golden-haired woman with wheat-like hair, geese flying around/out of her white cape. Egg imagery connects to Carrington's symbolism.

images.caption: The Giantess (The Guardian of the Egg)

clues.clue: Massive golden-haired giantess with geese flying from her white cape; egg symbolism
examples: a figure with a "moonlike face," golden wheat-like hair, and geese flying out of her white cape
painted birds flying out of the white cloak of a huge golden-haired woman

clues.clue: Painted in tempera (egg tempera); used eggs to depict eggs
examples: This artist used eggs to depict eggs in her tempera paintings The Giantess

clues.clue: Three small women may symbolize Carrington, Varo, and Horna
examples: Three small women may symbolize Carrington, Varo, and Horna in Carrington's painting of a giantess "guarding" this object

works.name: Portrait of Max Ernst (Bird Superior)
works.description: Full title: Bird Superior — Portrait of Max Ernst. Depicts Ernst in a snowy/frozen landscape wearing a red furry coat with a mermaid tail; a frozen white horse stands behind him. The 'Bird Superior' subtitle reflects Ernst's Surrealist alter-ego Loplop.

clues.clue: Man in a red coat with a mermaid tail stands in front of a frozen landscape with a white horse; also subtitled 'Bird Superior' (Ernst's Loplop alter-ego)
examples: A man wearing a red coat with a mermaid tail stands in front of a white horse in one work by this artist, which is set in a frozen landscape
a romantic partner in a red woolen coat with a mermaid's tail and a striped sock
A frozen white horse appears behind a man in a red coat in a Leonora Carrington portrait that depicts her lover as one of these animals 'Superior'

works.name: How Doth the Little Crocodile
works.description: Both a painting and bronze sculpture inspired by Lewis Carroll poem. Crocodiles rowing a boat, swimming through galaxies.

clues.clue: Both a painting and bronze sculpture inspired by Lewis Carroll; crocodiles rowing a boat through galaxies
examples: a sculpture and a painting by this artist inspired by Lewis Carroll depict a group of crocodiles rowing a boat
showed crocodiles swimming through a background of galaxies

works.name: The Hearing Trumpet (novel)
works.description: Surrealist novel in which 90-year-old Marian Leatherby uses the title device to eavesdrop on family plans to send her to a retirement home (which turns out to be a religious cult run by the Well of Light Brotherhood). Set in Mexico. David Lodge analyzed it in his essay 'Surrealism.' Ali Smith wrote an introduction praising how it 'rewrites our expectations of old ladies.' The protagonist acquires the device while praying to the planet Venus.

clues.clue: Novel: 90-year-old Marian Leatherby eavesdrops on family's plan to send her to a retirement home using the title device; retirement home is a cult run by Well of Light Brotherhood
examples: wrote a novel in which Marian Leatherby eavesdrops on her family's plans to send her to a retirement home by using the title device
the protagonist is sent to an old people's home which turns out to be a religious cult run by the Well of Light Brotherhood

clues.clue: Set in Mexico; protagonist acquires the title device while praying to Venus; during an ice age, she receives visitors including a wolf-headed sister named Anubeth and immortal postman Taliesin
examples: Marian Leatherby acquires one of these instruments while praying to the planet Venus

comprehensive_summary:
Leonora Carrington (1917–2011) was a British-born Surrealist painter and author who did most of her work in Mexico. Her Self-Portrait / Inn of the Dawn Horse (1937–1938) shows her sitting in a blue chair reaching toward a lactating hyena, with a rocking horse on the wall and a white horse galloping through the window symbolizing her freedom. Her short story 'The Debutante' features a girl who switches places with a talking hyena. The Giantess (The Guardian of the Egg) is a tempera painting of a massive golden-haired woman with geese flying from her white cape — three small women in it may symbolize Carrington, Remedios Varo, and Kati Horna.

Her Portrait of Max Ernst (subtitled Bird Superior, referencing Ernst's alter-ego Loplop) depicts her lover in a frozen landscape wearing a red coat with a mermaid tail beside a white horse. Both Carrington and Meret Oppenheim had affairs with Ernst, who pioneered frottage and grattage. How Doth the Little Crocodile, inspired by Lewis Carroll, exists as both a painting and bronze sculpture showing crocodiles in galaxies. The Magical World of the Maya is a mural for the National Museum of Anthropology that depicts a wide rainbow underneath an orange sky. She also painted The Temptation of St. Anthony, in which the central man is very old, three-headed, and wears an eggshell-like hood.

Carrington suffered a mental breakdown in Spain after Ernst's arrest and was institutionalized, experiences she documented in the memoir Down Below (stomach became 'mirror of the earth'; drugged with cardiazol, deemed 'incurably insane'). She wrote The Hearing Trumpet, a surrealist novel about 90-year-old Marian Leatherby who acquires the title device while praying to Venus, eavesdrops on family plans to commit her, and ends up in a cult run by the Well of Light Brotherhood. During an ice age she receives visitors including wolf-headed Anubeth and immortal postman Taliesin. David Lodge examined the novel in his essay 'Surrealism'; Ali Smith wrote an introduction. Her short story collection is titled for 'The Seventh Horse.'

She grew up at the Gothic mansion Crookhey Hall, calling her father 'Lord Candlestick.' In Mexico City, she was part of the 'three witches' with Remedios Varo and Kati Horna — they brewed Surrealist potions and played exquisite corpse. She left Mexico temporarily in 1968 because of the Tlatelolco massacre. She illustrated Elena Poniatowska's Lilus Kikus. Tarot cards she created were discovered by Tere Arcq for a 2018 retrospective and published by Fulgur Press in 2021. Her play Baa-Lamb's Holiday was adapted into a multimedia opera by Olga Neuwirth and Elfriede Jelinek. Late in life she pivoted to sculpture (encouraged by Isaac Masri): Godiva in the Jungle shows a backward-facing woman atop a tapir. The 2022 Venice Biennale's main exhibition 'The Milk of Dreams' honored her.
